title: DF-006: Preserve notification 404 errors
brief: Fix the backend notification service paths that intentionally raise HTTP 404 for missing notifications but then catch their own HTTPException in a broad exception handler and return HTTP 500.

criteria.statement: Updating a missing single notification preserves the intended HTTP 404 instead of returning HTTP 500.
criteria.verification: Focused async unit test calls NotificationService.update_single_notification with no matching notification and asserts HTTP 404.

criteria.statement: Bulk-editing missing notifications preserves the intended HTTP 404 instead of returning HTTP 500.
criteria.verification: Focused async unit test calls NotificationService.bulk_edit_notifications with an empty query result and asserts HTTP 404.

criteria.statement: Unexpected exceptions in the same paths still return HTTP 500.
criteria.verification: Focused async unit tests inject unexpected database errors and assert HTTP 500.

coordination.messageTopology: Foreman-mediated handoff; OPE-24, run manifest, ledger events, and gate files are the source of truth.
coordination.authorityDistribution: Foreman owns stage advancement, evidence checks, No Mistakes, push, and PR commands.
coordination.syncMode: bounded async review with typed verdict files
coordination.aggregation: typed PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL/BLOCKED verdicts with evidence refs
coordination.termination: stop on PASS, budget exhaustion, or BLOCKED escalation
coordination.failureHandling: retry inside loop budget, then escalate without pretending success
coordination.informationPolicy: builder sees the frozen contract; hidden holdouts stay with the independent judge
coordination.computeAccounting: record model/tool usage estimates as evidence, not billing truth

loop.judge: Claude Opus 4.8 max independent review after Codex build

loops.trigger: Immediately after the planning team returns a plan and before implementation starts, push back to the planning team exactly once and ask: Is there anything else that has been missed? Are there any errors? Are there any conflicts in the plan?
loops.judge: Foreman verifies the one planning-team challenge was sent once and the response or lack of response was recorded before implementation proceeds.
loops.memory: Ticket comments, Foreman loop event, run manifest, and evidence/source of truth for the planning-team response or lack of response.
loops.exitCondition: The one adversarial plan challenge is recorded with the planning team response or lack of response.
loops.escalation: If the challenge finds missed work, errors, or conflicts, return to the planning team for correction before implementation proceeds.

risk.reason: Small error-preservation fix in notification update paths; no database schema, authorization policy, vendor integration, or UI behavior change.

taskRoute.decision: This WorkOrder is explicitly routed to the legal/dev app under aila-code, not main app stage.
